heading: Office of administrative assistant and disbursing officer in Library of Congress abolished; transfer of duties to appointee of Librarian
label: 142a Office of administrative assistant and disbursing officer in Library of Congress abolished; transfer of duties to appointee of Librarian
text: From and after , the office of administrative assistant and disbursing officer of the Library of Congress, created by , is abolished and thereafter the duties required to be performed by the administrative assistant and disbursing officer shall be performed, under the direction of the Librarian of Congress, by such persons as the Librarian may appoint for those purposes.June 10, 1928section 142 of this title
notes: References in Text, referred to in text, was omitted from the Code.Section 142 of this title
Amendments1972— struck out provisions which required the person disbursing appropriations for Library of Congress and Botanic Garden to give a bond in sum of $30,000.Pub. L. 92–310

Transfer of FunctionsDisbursement functions of all Government agencies, except Departments of the Army, Navy, and Air Force and Panama Canal transferred to Division of Disbursements, Treasury Department, by Ex. Ord. No. 6166, § 4, , and Ex. Ord. No. 6728, .June 10, 1933May 29, 1934Division subsequently consolidated with other agencies into Fiscal Service in Treasury Department by Reorg. Plan No. III of 1940, § 1(a)(1), eff. , 5 F.R. 2107, . See , Money and Finance.June 30, 194054 Stat. 1231section 306 of Title 31
